title: Site visit: EnergyX launches first US direct lithium extraction plant in Texas
summary: At a packed event in Hooks, Texas, EnergyX this week unveiled a first-of-its-kind lithium production facility — project Lonestar — marking a significant step toward establishing a scalable domestic supply of battery-grade lithium in the United States. The company’s now operational demonstration plant is capable of producing approximately 250 metric tons per year of lithium carbonate equivalent (LCE). While modest in output on a global scale, the facility represents a critical validation of EnergyX’s proprietary direct li...

title: Wells Fargo doubles down on gold price despite slump
summary: Wells Fargo has doubled down on gold despite the metal’s recent slump that had some worried of a reversal into bear-market territory. In their latest investment research report, the bank’s analysts led by Edward Lee reiterated gold’s long-term bull thesis, citing the same factors that had supported...

title: This Day in Alaska History-March 29, 1867
summary: It was on March 29th, 1867, that the stirrings began that would change this great northern land into the territory then state of Alaska. It was on this day that the Russian minister to the U.S., Eduard Andreevich Stoeckl, rushed to Secretary of State William H. Seward to bring him important news fro...

title: AST Theft: Fairbanks [AK26027432]
summary: On 03/29/2026 at approximately 0929 hours, Alaska State Troopers in Fairbanks received a report of a theft of prescription medication from a residence in Fairbanks. The investigation is ongoing.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST DUI: Soldotna [AK26027403]
summary: On 03/28/2026, at approximately 1919 hours AST, Soldotna dispatch received a REDDI (Report Every Dangerous Driver Immediately) report at approximately the 20-mile post of Kalifornsky Beach Rd. of a tan Toyota Sequoia driving all over the road, driving at varying speeds, opening up his door, and throwing up while the vehicle was in motion. Troopers contacted the driver, Ilya Torchinsky (48y/o) of Nikiski. Troopers initiated a DUI investigation, which led to the arrest of Ilia. Ilia transported Ilya to Wildwood Pretrial and remanded him on driving while under the influence (AS28.35.030(a)(2).
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST DV Assault: Nikiski [AK26027423]
summary: On March 29, 2026, at approximately 0144 hours, the Alaska State Troopers received a report of an active assault in Nikiski. AST responded and investigation resulted in the arrest of Kevin Demspey (28 y/o) of Nikiski. Kevin was remanded at Wildwood Correctional Complex for Assault 4 DV and Assault in the Presence of a Child.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Probation Remand: Big Lake [AK26027412]
summary: On 03/28/2026 AST received a report from Brett Maybury's (age 31, of Wasilla) court-ordered third-party custodian that they did not want to be his custodian anymore. Brett also called AST, asking to be remanded into custody. Brett was transported to MSPT without incident.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST DUI, MIW: Wasilla [AK26027422]
summary: On 03/29/2026 at approximately 0108 hours, Alaska State Troopers responded to a 911 hang-up followed shortly by a rapid SOS automatic crash notification. Following the investigation, John Jaques, age 52 of Palmer, was arrested for DUI and Misconduct involving weapons in the fourth degree. Jaques was remanded to Mat-Su Pretrial and held on an OR bail per the bail schedule.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

charges: Misconduct involving weapons in the fourth degree

title: Republican legislators wanted to ban stock market investments by the Permanent Fund
summary: Fifty years ago last week, the state House of Representatives approved its version of the Permanent Fund amendment to the Alaska Constitution on a 36-1 vote. Here is a clear recording of the historic debate that day. It begins about 8 minutes into this recording and lasts for less than 25 minutes. Most of the voices you hear are from great public servants who are no longer with us, but deserve to be remembered as among the many fathers of the Permanent Fund, including Hugh Malone, Clark Gruening and Mike Bradner. Terry Gardiner, who was 25 at the time, is still hale and hearty. The vote that day, though it was not the final legislative action, was one of the most important in Alaska his...

title: Governor’s race survey: the PFD question. (And ferries.)
summary: Northern Journal’s survey of Alaska’s many gubernatorial hopefuls is back — this time, asking how much money candidates would budget for Permanent Fund dividends. We also find out when each candidate last rode on Alaska’s state ferry system — a publicly funded network that’s been the subject of polarizing budget debates in recent years. Starting in this edition, we’ve narrowed the surveyed candidates to those who reported raising at least $3,000 in the latest round of campaign finance reports — as well as those who announced their candidacies after the latest reporting deadline. Republicans Bernadette Wilson, Adam Crum, Treg Taylor and Nancy Dahlstrom did not respond to our questions; we sent them multiple requests. Have suggestions for future survey questions? Email nat[at]northernjournal[dot]com. Links to previous editions of the survey: schools funding , Alaska’s LNG project and oil taxes . Question 1 Gov. Mike Dunleavy proposed a Permanent Fund dividend amount for the coming fiscal...

title: Governor’s race survey: How much money would you propose for schools? And: cars.
summary: We’re back with a new edition of our recurring survey of the more than dozen candidates running to be Alaska’s next governor. This week’s survey explores a subject that has commanded huge amounts of political debate and discussion at Alaska’s Capitol in recent years: How much money should the state share with school districts on a per-student basis? We also asked about what kind of car each candidate drives — because a car can tell you a lot about a person. Republicans Bernadette Wilson, Nancy Dahlstrom, Adam Crum, Treg Taylor and independent Jessica Faircloth did not respond to the survey despite multiple requests. Read the previous editions of the survey, on Alaska’s LNG project and oil taxes . Have suggestions for future survey questions? Email nat[at]northernjournal[dot]com. Question 1 The Alaska Legislature last year passed a law boosting the state’s baseline per-student schools spending by some 12% to $6,660, up from $5,960, and overrode Republican Gov. Mike Dunleavy’s veto of th...

title: Governor’s race survey: Should Alaska spend millions more on its huge LNG project? Plus, Alaska books.
summary: We’re back with the next edition of our recurring survey of the more than a dozen candidates running to be Alaska’s next governor. This week’s questions revolve around a huge — and hugely expensive — LNG export project pushed by Alaska’s current governor, Mike Dunleavy. We also asked for some book recommendations. Full questions and answers are below. Republican candidates Bernadette Wilson and Treg Taylor did not respond to the survey; Northern Journal sent multiple messages to each. The previous edition of the survey, on oil taxes and fish recipes, is here . Have suggestions for future survey questions? Email nat[at]northernjournal[dot]com. Question 1 The Alaska LNG export project has been discussed and debated for decades, but in spite of huge sums of state money spent on it, construction has not begun. Gov. Mike Dunleavy says the project is as close to a final investment decision as it’s ever been; skeptics point out that no customers have signed binding contracts to purchase gas a...

title: AST MIW3rd (x5) and MIW5th: Healy [AK26027419]
summary: On 03/28/2026 at 1710 hours the Alaska State Troopers conducted a traffic stop for a moving violation near Mile 248 Parks Highway in Healy. Investigation determined the driver, Brian Reagin, age 50 from Fairbanks was a convicted felon and was in possession of 5 handguns. Reagin also failed to disclose he was concealing a handgun on his person when contacted by law enforcement. Brian was arrested and remanded to FCC where he was held without bail pending arraignment for 5 counts of Misconduct Involving Weapons 3rd and one count of Misconduct Involving Weapons 5th.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: near Mile 248 Parks Highway in Healy
